# Design Principles {#sec-design}

A reference implementation and proof-of-concept for offline, SLSA‑compliant AOSP builds. The system is modular — individual components (attestation, FHS environment, credential storage) can be adapted or replaced for specific distribution needs.

Core properties:

* **Portable** — Runs on arbitrary `x86_64` hardware with UEFI, ≥250 GB disk, ≥64 GB memory.
* **Offline** — Requires no network beyond internal source and artifact repositories.
* **Ephemeral** — Each boot produces a pristine environment; no trace remains after shutdown.
* **Declarative** — All aspects are described in Nix expressions, ensuring identical behavior regardless of build environment or time.
* **Trusted** — Disk images are cryptographically signed. TPM-based attestation verifies the running system.

## Limitations

* **aarch64 support** — only `x86_64` with UEFI is implemented.
* **Artifact uploads** — build artifacts can be persisted to a second disk but are not automatically uploaded to remote storage.
* **Configuration** — adapting to custom AOSP distributions requires familiarity with NixOS modules. See @sec-options-reference.

## Technologies

* **NixOS / nixpkgs** — declarative system configuration, reproducible builds
* **systemd** — service orchestration, `systemd-repart` for disk images, `systemd-creds` for TPM-encrypted credentials
* **LUKS + dm-verity** — disk encryption (ephemeral + TPM2-bound) and read-only store integrity
* **erofs** — compressed read-only filesystem for the immutable store partition
* **Keylime** — TPM-based remote attestation (Rust agent, Python registrar/verifier)
* **YubiKey / PAM** — hardware-token authentication via `pam_u2f`
* **USBGuard** — USB device blocking during unattended builds
* **QEMU** — VM-based testing

An SBOM for the builder closure can be generated:

```bash
nix run github:tiiuae/sbomnix#sbomnix -- .#nixosConfigurations.nixos.toplevel
```

## Deployment Topology

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-deployment-topology
%%| fig-width: 6
%%| fig-cap: "Deployment topology"
graph LR
    local["Local Machine<br/><i>nix build, sign</i>"]
    server["Attestation Server<br/><i>registrar, verifier,<br/>auto-enroll, git (opt)</i>"]
    builder["Builder Machine<br/><i>keylime-agent, TPM 2.0</i>"]

    local -- "flash via USB" --> builder
    builder -- "register (EK)" --> server
    server <-- "TPM quotes" --> builder
```

- The **local machine** builds and signs disk images (requires only Nix).
- The **attestation server** runs keylime services, optionally via system-manager on non-NixOS. See @sec-attestation-server.
- The **builder machine** boots the signed image, registers with the server, and runs AOSP builds.
